title: ‘Thank you for 8 great years!’: Fenway Johnnie’s has closed
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Fenway Johnnie's closed after 8 years of operation near Fenway Park, as announced on their Facebook page.
Located at 96 Brookline Ave., the sports bar offered items like potato skins, burgers, and Fenway sausage.
West End Johnnie's, the original location, will remain open, continuing the restaurant's presence in Boston.

title: Jayson Tatum reacts to Duke’s buzzer-beater loss to UConn: ‘I was sick’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum expressed his disappointment at Duke's Elite Eight loss to UConn via a buzzer-beater during March Madness.
Tatum, a former Duke player and current chief basketball officer, praised the team's season despite their tournament exit.
UConn's win over Duke advances them to the Final Four, aiming for a third national title in four years.

title: Boston man charged with sexually assaulting MBTA bus driver
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Michael Vandenbroeke, 41, charged with indecent assault and battery, allegedly assaulted MBTA bus driver on March 26.
The alleged incident occurred on a Silver Line bus in Boston's South End and was recorded on surveillance video.
Vandenbroeke has a history of violent offenses dating back to 2005; he was held on $500 bail.

title: The best movies on Netflix right now (April 2026)
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Ralph Fiennes stars in '28 Years Later: The Bone Temple,' a new release and sequel in the '28 Days Later' series.
'Atonement' featuring James McAvoy and Keira Knightley explores an ill-fated romance during World War II.
Tom Cruise's 'Mission: Impossible' (1996) kicks off the franchise with cerebral action, setting a template for future films.

title: Kelsey Fitzsimmons was found not guilty. Now she’s suing.
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Kelsey Fitzsimmons, a former North Andover police officer, was found not guilty of assault after being shot by a colleague.
Fitzsimmons plans to sue town officials, alleging civil rights violations and negligence related to the shooting incident.
The lawsuit questions actions during the restraining order service, including allowing her ex-fiancé into her home.

title: A church turned 6-BR home in Nahant — can you guess the price?
author: Emily Turner
bullets: A converted church in Nahant, built in 1851, is now a six-bedroom home with ocean views.
The property includes 5,969 square feet, three full baths, and an in-law apartment.
The listing price for the converted church ranges from $1.6 million to $2 million.

title: Should I DIY repair damage to a brand-new vehicle?
author: John Paul
bullets: DIY repair of a damaged side mirror on a 2026 Honda CR-V Sport is possible but may affect lease responsibilities.
John Paul advises that leaving the mirror damage open shouldn't affect electronics due to robustness against weather.
Color-matching factory paint is available online, and DIY solutions like molding or 3-D printing can be considered.

title: A nude man was seen walking around a N.H. town overnight. Police would like to know who he is.
author: John Waller
bullets: Police in Bedford, NH, seek to identify a nude man seen walking around neighborhoods early Thursday morning.
The man reportedly walked up several driveways between 2 a.m. and 3 a.m., prompting police investigation.
The suspect may match the description from previous reports of a nude man running on Campbell Road.

title: This nurse is running Boston for MGH — and to honor her grandfather
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Abigail Zerbey, 24, from North Andover, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon in memory of her grandfather.
Zerbey's grandfather received treatment for pancreatic cancer at Massachusetts General Hospital, where she now works as a nurse.
She is raising funds for MGH's patient care, pediatric cancer research, and the Home Base program through her marathon run.

title: Heat coach Erik Spoelstra credited Celtics’ ‘exceptional level of shot-making’
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Celtics' first-quarter 53 points led to a 147-129 victory over Miami Heat.
Heat coach Erik Spoelstra praised Celtics' high-efficiency shooting, especially from Jaylen Brown and Sam Hauser.
Boston excelled inside the arc, shooting nearly 60% on 2-pointers, while Heat managed only 41.7%.
